channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Senate To Consult IMF On Nigeria’s Economic Plans And Reforms
item.description: The Nigerian Senate don arrange high-level meeting with IMF officials as part of discussions on Nigeria’s economic situation under President Bola Tinubu. Dis one dey under IMF Article IV Consultation,...

item.title: Understanding LPG Usage in Cooking Cylinders and Alternatives with PNG
item.description: The article discusses why liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) is predominantly used in cooking gas cylinders in India, and whether piped natural gas (PNG) can serve as a viable alternative. LPG has been the...

item.title: Statistics Canada to release latest February employment and unemployment report
item.description: Statistics Canada is preparing to release its official February 2026 jobs and unemployment data, a key indicator for understanding the country’s economic direction as it faces ongoing global uncertain...

item.title: Heavy Rain Forces Cancellation of SS3 Stage in Safari Rally
item.description: The organisers of the 2026 WRC Safari Rally have cancelled the SS3 Camp Moran 2 stage that was meant to open Day Two of the rally due to heavy rains that made the route impassable. Instead, the action...

item.title: Simba SC secures 2–1 win over Singida Black Stars with strong team effort
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Simba SC head coach Steve Barker expressed his happiness with his players after they fought hard to win 2–1 against Singida Black Stars in a Mainland Premier League match at Airtel Sta...

item.title: Advice for US Ambassador Bozell on Respecting South African Political Norms
item.description: Shortly after taking up his post, US Ambassador Leo Brent Bozell III sparked controversy in South Africa due to remarks perceived as disrespectful towards the country's courts. The opinion piece by Re...

item.title: Government assures steady LPG supply and urges citizens to avoid panic buying
item.description: On Friday, the Government of India conducted a joint inter-ministerial briefing for the third consecutive day to address public concerns over LPG cylinder supplies amidst the ongoing West Asia conflic...

item.title: Benjamin Mkapa Stadium to host one-off Azam FC vs Yanga match during renovations
item.description: The Ministry of Information, Culture, Arts and Sports has clarified that Benjamin Mkapa Stadium will be used temporarily for the upcoming match between Azam FC and Young Africans S.C. this Sunday. Acc...

item.title: Senators Bong Go and Bato dela Rosa and the Question of Senate Sanctuary Amid ICC Cases
item.description: Senators Bong Go and Bato dela Rosa, both associated with the Philippine drug war under former President Duterte, may invoke the Senate's traditional sanctuary if Interpol attempts to arrest them in c...

item.title: US launches investigation into foreign governments over forced labour imports
item.description: The United States Trade Representative’s office has announced Section 301 investigations into 60 countries for allegedly failing to prevent goods produced with forced labour from entering the US. The ...

item.title: Sunrisers Leeds sign Pakistani spinner Abrar Ahmed amid social media criticism
item.description: Sunrisers Leeds, an Indian-owned franchise in the Hundred cricket competition, have recently signed Pakistani leg-spinner Abrar Ahmed for a fee of $252,000. This move has sparked widespread criticism ...

item.title: Sea Cliff Golf Club launches successful junior golf training in Zanzibar
item.description: The Sea Cliff Resort and Spa Golf Club in Zanzibar has recently started a Junior Golf Training Programme which is already seeing good participation. Just two weeks after the launch, 11 children aged b...

item.title: A London initiative uses outdoor experiences to support mental health
item.description: Dose of Nature, a charity in London, is pioneering a novel approach to mental health treatment by prescribing time spent outdoors. Founded by former NHS psychologist Alison Greenwood, the project has ...

item.title: Broda Shaggi Hospitalised After Alleged Shooting During Skit Filming
item.description: Popular Nigerian comedian and social media influencer, Broda Shaggi, whose real name na Samuel Perry, don end up for hospital after e reportedly get shot while dem dey film comedy skit for Sango-Ota, ...

item.title: Debate Over Natural vs. Human-Assisted Recovery of Giant Sequoias After Wildfire
item.description: In the Redwood Mountain Grove of Sequoia & Kings Canyon National Parks, tens of thousands of giant sequoia seedlings and saplings have emerged after the devastating 2021 KNP Complex fire. While the ma...

item.title: Government sets up ₹1 lakh crore Economic Stabilisation Fund to manage global economic uncertainties
item.description: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman, during the Lok Sabha debate on the second batch of supplementary demands for grants, announced the creation of a ₹1 lakh crore Economic Stabilisation Fund. The pur...

item.title: DJ Mo Attacked by Thieves in Kenol, Size 8 Thanks Friends for His Rescue
item.description: Kenyan gospel musician-turned-pastor Size 8 has expressed her devastation after her husband, popular DJ Mo, was attacked by thieves while in Kenol. The robbers reportedly used a panga during the assau...

item.title: Seven Tanzanians rescued from Iran return safely home
item.description: Dar es Salaam witnessed an emotional homecoming as seven Tanzanian nationals, who had been stuck in Iran amid escalating regional tensions, arrived safely at Julius Nyerere International Airport (JNIA...

item.title: Estranged couple dispute finances in R1.5m divorce settlement
item.description: The estranged wife of a former Bafana Bafana and Orlando Pirates midfielder has filed a legal application claiming that her ex-husband has not disclosed his full financial details in relation to a pro...

item.title: Rick Morton critiques NACC’s robodebt report and its handling of key figures
item.description: In a sharply worded analysis, journalist and author Rick Morton examines the National Anti-Corruption Commission’s (NACC) report into the robodebt scandal, which controversially cleared former prime m...
